risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "We will randomly allocate practices to the intervention or usual care arm. We will pair practices by size ... and by whether they have implemented the DIAMOND ... program. Within each pair, we will randomize practices to either: 1. the use of Depression Medication Choice ... or 2. usual care. A study statistician will perform the randomization centrally after the practices have been enrolled and ensure concealment of allocation." Comment: Although the protocol does not specify the exact random number method, it explicitly states that practices will be randomized within matched pairs and that a study statistician will perform the randomization centrally. In Cochrane assessment, randomization within pairs is acceptable here because the sequence generation is described as random and there is no indication of a non-random or predictable method.

Quote: "A study statistician will perform the randomization centrally after the practices have been enrolled and ensure concealment of allocation." Comment: This explicitly describes central allocation and states that allocation concealment was ensured, which is sufficient to judge low risk for foreknowledge of upcoming assignment.

risk_of_bias.domain: Blinding of participants and personnel (performance bias)
risk_of_bias.support_text: Quote: "Practices, clinicians and investigators will not be masked to allocation." Comment: Participants and the clinicians delivering the intervention were aware of group assignment, and the main outcomes included subjective measures (e.g. decisional conflict, satisfaction, knowledge, depression outcomes, and medication adherence) that could plausibly be influenced by knowledge of allocation and by the intervention being used during the consultation.

Quote: "Practices, clinicians and investigators will not be masked to allocation." and "We will collect patients’ data through: 1. self-reported questionnaires administered before and after the clinical encounter with their clinician, and at 3 and 6 months post-encounter"; "Patients will complete the decisional conflict scale (DCS) immediately after the clinical encounter"; "Patients will complete the nine items of the PHQ-9"; "Patient, clinician and practice socio-demographic information will be collected through questionnaires". Comment: The main outcomes here are largely patient self-reported and clinician-rated questionnaire outcomes, so the patient/clinician who provides the rating is the assessor. Because the trial explicitly states that investigators were not masked to allocation and does not report blinded outcome assessment, knowledge of group assignment could influence subjective outcomes such as decisional conflict, knowledge, satisfaction, and self-reported depression symptoms.

Quote: "To prevent loss to follow-up and support the intention-to-treat principle, we will centrally follow patients, and ensure that patient surveys and pharmacy follow-ups are complete." Comment: This protocol indicates active measures to minimize missing outcome data and explicitly plans intention-to-treat analysis with centralized follow-up for patient surveys and pharmacy data. Although exact attrition numbers are not yet reported because this is a trial protocol, the stated procedures suggest missing data are unlikely to bias results.
